blocks.content: As Shirone burst into laughter, Zion's face turned red.
A lowly commoner looking down on him and laughing? He had never experienced such humiliation in his life.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: What did he mean by no sons? Only daughters? Then who was he? Was there such a thing as a child without parents?
Zion's face twisted like a wounded beast.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Shut up! How would someone like you know that? You're insane. You've gone mad after being consumed by that monster! Who are you to speak about my mother like that?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Didn't Teraje tell you? You must have been a truly pathetic fool. She respects ability above all else, even if you're not her child. But it seems you weren't even worth that.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: When did you meet my mother?
Zion, losing his temper, charged.
He had lived his whole life with the pride of being Teraje's son. And now he was being called a shell?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: No, it was the ramblings of a madman. How could a commoner like him have ever met Teraje, the emperor of the continent?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: I am royalty! A bastard like you has no right to speak to me!
As the blade of Zion's gauntlet approached Shirone's neck, a photon cannon struck Zion's abdomen.
Zion spat out a mouthful of blood and was thrown 10 meters into a sack of flour.
Cough! Cough!
The sack tore open, and flour poured over Zion like a waterfall.
His face, covered in flour and blood, looked like that of a clown.
Armand reported: Combat ineffective.
It was the only information Zion could comprehend.
Shirone walked over, holding a photon cannon in his palm.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Humans love such things. Kings, bloodlines, heritage, families. But Teraje, whom you follow, once said this to me.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Shirone mimicked Teraje's tone.
Shirone's photon cannon grew stronger.
Half out of his mind, Zion seemed unaware that it was a spell meant to attack him. He stared at the light with a dazed expression.
Shirone grinned, holding the photon cannon.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Talent is bestowed by the heavens.
The photon cannon fell in a brutal arc.
Tears streamed down Zion's face as he watched the light's trajectory in despair.
A powerful shockwave shook the food storage.
The photon cannon, buried in Armand's fibers, tore through Zion's organs until it exhausted all its power and dissipated.
Flour spread in a ring, creating a thick fog.
Amy and Reina anxiously scanned the white smoke.
A shadow appeared, and Shirone emerged.
Amy couldn't bring herself to speak.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Even if that boy was Shirone, the aura emanating from him felt like holding a ticking time bomb—dangerous and unstable.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm going to the surface. You need to run as fast and as far as you can. I'll handle things here.
The moment of truth had arrived.
Run fast and far? Such words were only spoken in the face of natural disasters like typhoons or earthquakes.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What are you planning to do? Whoever you are, you need to treat your injuries. That body belongs to Shirone!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I am Shirone.
Shirone tilted his head back to look at the ceiling.
His cold gaze slowly descended to Amy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Are you disappointed?
Amy couldn't breathe, unable to say a word.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone channeled his immense rage to strengthen his mind.
The sound of blood vessels bursting was eerily audible beneath his skin.
As he cast a laser, a beam as thick as a log shot up to the ceiling.
The rapid accumulation of thermal energy caused the ceiling to shake.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone! Wait...!

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: It's dangerous! We need to run!
Reina grabbed Amy's wrist and ran for the exit.
As they left the food storage, an explosion echoed from Shirone's direction.
The shockwave sent them rolling on the ground.
They turned their heads to see a massive rock blocking the entrance to the food storage.
The court musicians' music turned discordant.
As an earthquake shook the grand hall, women screamed and collapsed.
The men also looked around in fear.

blocks.speaker: Orkamp
blocks.content: An earthquake? Kazra isn't on a fault line, is it?

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Everyone, get down! Get under the tables!
At the guards' shouts, the nobles crawled under the tables.
Those who got there first fought with those who arrived later, resembling a bullfight.
The earthquake lasted about a minute before stopping.
The nobles, regaining their composure, crawled out from under the tables with embarrassed expressions.
Orkamp and Eliza, supported by the guards, also looked pale.

blocks.speaker: Orkamp
blocks.content: There had never been an earthquake reported in Kazra's history.
A sense of foreboding crossed Orkamp's mind.
The north side of the grand hall exploded with a bang.
Bricks flew in all directions, and smoke filled the air.
A few nobles, already on edge, lost their composure. They fell to the ground, flailing their arms and legs meaninglessly.
The first to grasp the situation were the palace guards and the nobles' personal bodyguards.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: An attack? What's happening?
The guards searched for the head of security, Ritney Walker.
He had been there just moments ago but was nowhere to be found.
The chain of command was in disarray.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Commander
blocks.content: If it were terrorists from an enemy nation, the security level needed to be raised to the highest.
But there were too many factors to consider before making such a decision without orders from a superior.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Commander
blocks.content: For a moment, nothing happened.
The nobles tried to maintain their dignity. They hid their true feelings and peered into the hole in the floor.
But when a figure emerged, they fell back in shock.

blocks.speaker: Orkamp
blocks.content: Is that... Shirone?
Orkamp muttered with a puzzled expression.
The person who had wrecked the grand hall was none other than Shirone.
He remembered seeing Shirone dancing with Reina just an hour ago. Why was he doing this now?

blocks.speaker: Orkamp
blocks.content: Shirone, what on earth are you doing?
The guards blocked Orkamp's path.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Your Majesty, don't approach. Something is wrong.
Even Orkamp could see that Shirone looked different from an hour ago.
His eye color had changed, making him look like a different person, but the most striking difference was his expression.
Shirone never showed such emotional expressions. He always maintained a calm poker face, but now he looked pained and angry.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Modern castles are quite durable. Humans have come a long way.
A guard approached, ready to draw his sword.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Sir Shirone, using magic inside the castle is strictly forbidden!
Without turning, Shirone fired a photon cannon.
The guard dived and rolled on the floor. The photon cannon's beam chased him, scraping the marble floor.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: From now on, no one speaks without my permission. I make the decisions.
Shirone's seriousness was proven by his earlier attack.
If the guard had reacted a moment later, he would have been torn apart by the photon cannon.
The atmosphere in the grand hall turned icy.
The nobles looked around, confused and unable to understand.
For days, they had assessed Shirone as a herbivore—docile and honest, an easy prey.
But now, even the most experienced nobles found Shirone's presence ferocious and wild.
The guards, unable to find Walker, awaited orders from the deputy commander.
But someone who had always stayed out of responsibility couldn't make such a critical decision.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Deputy Commander, what should we do? Give us orders!
The deputy commander anxiously rolled his eyes.
The main forces were stationed outside the inner castle, so even if contacted, it would take time for them to arrive.
It was abnormal for an incident to occur in the heart of the castle, the grand hall.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Commander
blocks.content: The control device is a last resort. Why not leave it to us?
The deputy commander turned his head, feeling as if he had found a savior.
Kazra's head of magic, Aimar Bosun, was approaching.

blocks.speaker: Bosun
blocks.content: Ah, are you sure?

blocks.speaker: Bosun
blocks.content: We've been closely monitoring Shirone's movements. It seems he's finally showing his true colors. But we can handle it. With the security chief's permission, may we use magic?

blocks.speaker: Deputy Commander
blocks.content: Of course. If you could take care of it...
It was much better to share the responsibility than to bear it alone.
Besides, Bosun had significant influence in the palace, so he wouldn't ignore any fallout.
Bosun gave a fake smile and walked towards Shirone.
One by one, his subordinates emerged from the group of nobles and followed behind him.

blocks.speaker: Bosun
blocks.content: I'll stay by your side, sir.
The spot next to Bosun was taken by his closest aide, Mito Ekkashi.
Standing at 153 centimeters, with thick eyelids and eyes that slanted sharply, he had a childlike face.
Having roamed numerous battlefields since childhood, he had risen to the rank of certified 6th-level magician in Kazra's magic department at the age of twenty-three.
Of course, there were extreme geniuses like Olifer Shina, who became a certified 6th-level magician at the age of twenty.
But such individuals were rare, even on a kingdom scale, appearing perhaps once in a decade.
Ekkashi was certainly considered the future of Kazra.

blocks.speaker: Bosun
blocks.content: This is an unexpected opportunity, sir.

blocks.speaker: Bosun
blocks.content: Hmm, unexpected events can easily turn into disasters. But this time, you might be right.
Casting magic in the royal court was a serious crime.
Moreover, Shirone had attacked a royal guard and uttered treasonous words, effectively branding himself a condemned criminal.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, what has made the first prince candidate so angry?
Shirone glared coldly at Bosun.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: An hour ago, he had wanted to punch him. But now, he felt no such desire. He only wanted to kill him.
So, you're the one driving me mad.
Bosun's heart raced at the pure, 100% killing intent that didn't seem human.
But he didn't lose his composure.
He turned slightly and spoke to the royal guard.

blocks.speaker: Bosun
blocks.content: Escort His Majesty to safety. I'll handle this.
Understood. Your Majesty, please come.
Orkamp shook off the guard's hand.

blocks.speaker: Orkamp
blocks.content: Who said anything about running away? Who do you think is the king here?
I-I'm sorry!
Sensing his mistake, the guard knelt.
In the urgency of the situation, he had followed Bosun's words.
Under different circumstances, it might have been overlooked.
But considering Bosun was part of the Teraje faction, it was indeed a reckless move.
